title: Vegetable Korma
time: Prep Time: 25 min | Cook Time: 35 min

ingredients.title: For the vegetables
items: 1 cup carrots – diced into ½ inch cubes
1 cup French beans – cut into 1-inch pieces
½ cup green peas (fresh or frozen)

ingredients.title: For the korma base paste
items: ¼ cup melon seeds (optional but adds creaminess)
1 tbsp poppy seeds (optional, for nutty aroma)
3–4 green chilies – slit (adjust to spice preference)

ingredients.title: For the sauce
items: 3 tbsp ghee (clarified butter) – or 2 tbsp ghee + 1 tbsp neutral oil
1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color, mild heat)
½ cup full-fat yogurt – whisked smooth
1½ cups fresh tomato purée (ripe tomatoes, blended and strained)
1 cup coconut milk (or cream for richer taste)

ingredients.title: For garnish
items: A few saffron strands soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk (optional, for luxury finish)

instruction.title: Step 1 – Prepare the vegetables
steps: Boil water in a deep pot with a little salt.
Blanch carrots, beans, cauliflower, potatoes for 2–3 minutes until half-cooked.
Add peas in the last 30 seconds, then drain and keep aside.

instruction.title: Step 2 – Make the korma paste
steps: In a small pan, boil sliced onions in 1 cup water for 5–6 minutes (this reduces sharpness).
Drain, then blend onions with cashews, melon seeds, poppy seeds, ginger, garlic, and green chilies into a smooth paste. Add a little water if needed.

instruction.title: Step 3 – Build the sauce
steps: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottom pan over medium heat.
Add bay leaves, cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, and cumin seeds — let them release aroma (about 30 seconds).
Add the prepared korma paste, sauté for 3–4 minutes until it loses raw smell.
Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder; sauté briefly.
Lower heat, stir in yogurt slowly to avoid curdling. Cook 2 minutes.
Add tomato purée, cook until the oil starts separating (sign of proper bhunao).
Pour in coconut milk, stir well, and simmer for 5 minutes.

instruction.title: Step 4 – Finish the korma
steps: Add blanched vegetables, season with salt, mix gently so they stay intact.
Simmer for 5–6 minutes until vegetables are fully cooked and flavors meld.
Stir in garam masala and cream.
Optional: add saffron milk for an elevated aroma.

instruction.title: Step 5 – Serve
steps: Serve hot with butter naan, tandoori roti, or jeera rice.

chefTips: Boiling onions first gives a sweeter, creamier base without bitterness.
Coconut milk + cream balance richness and aroma.
Use low heat after adding yogurt to keep the sauce silky, not split.
For banquets, keep sauce and vegetables separate until final reheating, so colors stay bright.
